title: practical english usage - Part 195
content:
The telephone always rings when you are having a bath.

As I was walking down the street I saw Joe driving a Porsche.

situation, which  is/was going on when something else happens/happened.

We   can use all three words to introduce a longer ‘ background’  action or or 1  'backgrounds':        as,   when         while as, when or while. There are some differences.

To  talk about actions or situations that take place at the same time, we  can use 251  as,   when      and   while:     simultaneous          event    s For before as an adverb and preposition, ►    396-397.       For before and ever, ►    454.4.

Before beginning the book, she spent five years on research.

Please turn out all the lights before leaving the office.

In a formal style, we  often use the structure before + -ing.

5 before       .. .ing She left before I could ask for her phone number.

We'd better get out of here before your father catches us.

something stops them).

We   sometimes use before to talk about things that don’ t happen (because 4 before     things     that    don't    happen than the action of the main verb. This is unusual.) (Note that in sentences like the last, a past perfect tense can refer to a time later when I had completed my  sentence.) He went out before I had finished my sentence. (= ... before the moment when I have completed the report.) You can't go home before I've signed the report. (= ... before the moment emphasise the idea of completion.

In clauses with before, we  often use present perfect and past perfect tenses to 3  perfect      tenses We can't leave before the speeches have finished.

I'll telephone you before I come. (     ... before I will come.) (►  231).

With before, we  use a present or present perfect tense if the meaning is future 2 present      or  present      perfect      tense     with    future     meaning / have breakfast before I go for a walk. (The speaker has breakfast first.) has breakfast. Compare: Note the time relations: in both cases the speaker goes for a walk first and then Other Adverbial ClausesSection 23 grammar *251  as, when and while: simultaneous events For other uses of as, when and while, see the Index.

While in Germany, he got to know a family of musicians. (= While  he was ...) Climb when ready. (= ... when you are ready.) Don't forget to signal when turning right. (= ... when you are turning right.) means 'whenever'), and after while. This is rather formal.

It is often possible to leave out subject + be after when (especially when it 5 reduced      clauses      with    when      and   while We'll feel a lot better when we're lying on the beach next week.

I'll wash some clothes while you're out.

As you get used to the work you'll find it quite easy.

when talking about the future.

As with other time conjunctions, we  use present tenses with as, when and while 4 future     reference I thought of it just when you opened your mouth.

(Just) when is also possible.

Emily always arrives just as I start work.

As I opened my eyes I heard a strange voice.

happened at the same time.

We   usually use (just) as to say that two short actions or events happen/ 3 simultaneous          short    actions:     (just)   as;  (just)    when not His parents died when he was twelve, (     ... while-he was twelver) not When I was a child we lived in London, (     As/While I was a child...) We   prefer when to refer to ages and periods of life.

As I get older I get more optimistic.

change together.

As is used (with simple tenses) to talk about two situations which  develop or Jack cooked supper while I watched TV.

While you were reading the paper, 1 was working.

the same time. We   can use progressive or simple tenses.

We   usually use while to say that two longer actions or situations go/went on at 2 simultaneous         long    actions:     while;     as H/uvt the burglar broke into the house, they were playing cards.

(    AseWMe the telephone rung . . .) When the telephone rang I was having a hath.

action or event. As and while cannot be used in this way.
